title: Massaman Curry Paste

overview.description: A rich, aromatic Thai curry paste combining fresh herbs with warming whole spices. Batch cook and freeze for authentic Massaman curry.

ingredients: 10 dried long red chillies, deseeded and soaked in warm water for 20 minutes
2 stalks lemongrass, tender inner part only, sliced
method: Drain the soaked chillies, reserving the soaking water.
Toast the coriander seeds, cumin seeds, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, star anise and white peppercorns in a dry frying pan over a medium heat for 2–3 minutes until fragrant.
Remove the cinnamon from the pan and break into small pieces.
Remove the cardamom seeds from their pods and discard the pods.
Grind all the toasted spices to a fine powder.
Place the chillies, shallots, garlic, galangal, lemongrass, coriander stems, shrimp paste, ground spices, nutmeg and vegetable oil into a food processor or mini chopper.
Blend until a smooth, thick paste forms.
Scrape down the sides as required.
Add the reserved soaking water one tablespoon at a time only if needed to help the paste blend.
Use immediately or divide into portions and freeze.

learning.did-you-know: Massaman curry is believed to have been introduced to Thailand by Muslim traders and is one of the few Thai curries heavily influenced by Middle Eastern and Indian spices.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to combine fresh Thai aromatics with toasted whole spices to produce one of Thailand's most distinctive curry pastes.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A freezer-friendly curry paste that opens the door to authentic homemade Massaman curry with very little extra effort.
chapman-tips: Freeze in approximately 2 tablespoon portions—one portion makes a family curry for four.
Don't skip toasting the spices—it gives Massaman its signature depth of flavour.
Break the cinnamon stick into smaller pieces before grinding to help your grinder.
The aroma should be warm and fragrant rather than overwhelmingly spicy.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze for up to 6 months in silicone ice cube trays or small containers.
notes.leftovers: Can also be used as a marinade for slow-cooked beef or lamb.
